spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the sibling-index() and sibling-count() APIs, but active CSS style rules are syntax-broken, preventing standard execution.

interactive.rationale: Provides simple buttons to add/remove rows and dynamically balances the row listing.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Properly explains how sibling tree-counting avoids static nth-child rules.

edge_cases.rationale: JS capability check is correct, but the CSS @supports query is syntax-broken, making the CSS fallback unreliable.

references.rationale: Includes valid references to the spec and ChromeStatus.

design_system.rationale: Incorporates repository design tokens and transitions cleanly.

openQuestions.title: Fix syntax-broken CSS in sibling-index demo style block
openQuestions.detail: Lines 34 and 38 in sibling-index/index.html contain syntax errors (unbalanced parentheses and corrupt formulas), likely caused by a bad auto-replace. Fix them to match the documented code block: `background: hsl(calc(220 - (var(--i) - 1) * (220 / max(var(--n), 1))) 70% 35%);` and fix `@supports` rule.

summary: A simple and effective introduction to tree-counting sibling functions that is severely marred by a syntax-broken active CSS style block (lines 34 and 38), causing the background color gradient and CSS fallback queries to fail.
